commit.message:
Scribe: OB-Xd VST3 bridge integration session complete

- Orchestration logs: Spike (architecture), Jet (deployment), Faye (host diagnostics), Ed (verification), Coordinator (integration)
- Session log: Final bridge message-pump refactor validated
- Decision inbox merged: 6 entries covering IPlugFrame fix, versioned deployment, thread affinity root cause
- Cross-agent history updated: Team learnings documented
- Archive check: decisions.md at 152KB, entries all recent (no archiving needed)

Architectural invariant locked:
> The bridge main thread is the UI/STA thread. All window creation, COM operations, plugin loads, and IPlugView calls happen on this thread. Pipe reader is background thread only.

Validation complete:
✅ Shipped Debug bridge byte-matches rebuilt Release bridge
✅ load_ack succeeds; openEditor successfully calls attached() without deadlock
✅ OB-Xd editor incomplete UI confirmed as plugin-side (beyond minimal host scope)
✅ Versioned deployment + manifest working end-to-end
⚠️ Test coverage gap flagged: 0 automated tests currently cover VST3/UI behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add authority, methodology, and editorial foundation

- rewrite About page with author identity, project purpose, and trust cues
- add Methodology page covering data sources, extraction logic, scoring,
  filters, and site limitations
- add Articles section with two original explainers:
  * How Ticker Extraction Works
  * How to Read the Five Strategies
- strengthen homepage positioning around education and research
- rewrite strategy overview and all 5 strategy pages with clearer use
  cases, limits, and responsible framing
- add Methodology and Articles links to nav/footer
- extend Playwright page coverage for the new pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

proposes.

select a conversion rate and currency.

From Address | To Address | Amount | AmountTicker | Amount (USD) | Txn

commit.message:
Create complete Limbus Company parody browser 2D game

- index.html: 6 screens (menu/party/dungeon/battle/result/editor)
- css/: dark theme with red/gold accents, battle/dungeon/editor/party layouts
- js/data.js: GameData+GameState with all JSON embedded (file:// compatible)
- js/ui.js: sprite builder, damage popups, coin flip animation, notifications
- js/audio.js: Web Audio API tone-generated SFX (no external assets)
- js/battle.js: coin flip mechanic, speed-based turn order, HP persistence
- js/party.js: identity selection, party slot management
- js/dungeon.js: Canvas map rendering, node interactions, event/reward modals
- js/editor.js: tabbed editor for characters/dungeon/settings, import/export
- js/main.js: screen coordinator, full game flow orchestration
- data/: characters.json, enemies.json, dungeon.json, config.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add default style for StatusBar control (#4020)

* Add StatusBar style, brushes, and demo page

- Create MaterialDesignTheme.StatusBar.xaml with StatusBar, StatusBarItem, and Separator styles

Co-authored-by: Keboo <952248+Keboo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove hard-coded FontSize from StatusBar style to inherit from theme

Co-authored-by: Keboo <952248+Keboo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Consolidate Separator styles and remove redundant StatusBar separator brush

Centralizes the MaterialDesignSeparator style into a new resource dictionary and replaces the specific StatusBar.Separator brush with the general separator background brush. This refactor simplifies the theme definitions and ensures consistent separator styling across the StatusBar, Menu, and PopupBox components.

The change also updates the StatusBar demo with colored icons and explicit heights to better showcase the UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: compact draft master desktop loading layout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve OLED mode-switch freeze by eliminating render race condition

The display froze on every other centered-mode switch because
update_display() released _state_lock before rendering, then set
content_update_needed=False without the lock—clobbering the True
set by a concurrent set_mode() call from the encoder thread.

Changes:
- Hold _state_lock through entire update_display() render cycle
- Remove _snapshot_render_state() and snapshot-passing pattern
- Add _reset_scroll_state() helper (consolidates 5 scattered resets)
- Remove _clear_display() (bypassed composition, caused flash)
- Make _revert_to_default() atomic (one lock, not three)
- Add _advance_scroll_position() for lock-held scroll updates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix docstring references to removed value=None parameter

- Fix test_builtin_clamp_128_255_preset preset lookup filter

commit.message:
Add iTipFooty.com.au auto-tipping integration

Automates submitting prediction engine picks to iTipFooty:

- Login via POST to services/login.php with session cookie management
- Parse tipping page HTML to extract game numbers, team names, form fields
- Map prediction engine picks (team IDs) to iTipFooty H/A selections
- Submit tips via POST to services/SubmitTips.php
- Handle locked games (already kicked off) gracefully

Backend:
- New service: backend/src/services/itipfooty.ts
- New route: /api/itipfooty/status and /api/itipfooty/submit
- Credentials loaded from .env (ITIPFOOTY_USERNAME, PASSWORD, COMP_ID)
- dotenv added for env var loading
- Submission logged to DataSourceLog

Frontend:
- Settings page: iTipFooty Integration card with status display
- Submit Tips button with detailed result feedback per game
- Shows configuration instructions when not configured

Security:
- Credentials stored in backend/.env (gitignored)
- .env.example provided with empty placeholders
- Status endpoint masks username with ***

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude (#3100)

* fix: use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude

The recipe runner and delegation scripts hardcoded 'claude' as the agent
binary, causing failures when amplihack is run with other agents (copilot,
codex, etc.).

Changes:
- cli.py: Set AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var at dispatch for all 5 agent cmds
- rust_runner.py: Forward --agent-binary flag to Rust recipe runner
- platform_cli.py: Read env var instead of hardcoding 'claude'
- delegate_response.py (3 mirrors): Read env var + add missing 'import os'

The Rust recipe runner companion changes are in
rysweet/amplihack-recipe-runner#29.

Fixes #3099

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review findings - empty string edge case and missed hardcoded files

- Fix orchestrator.py: use ${AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY:-claude} bash fallback
- Fix executor.py (2 locations): same bash fallback pattern for remote execution
- Fix empty string edge case: use 'or "claude"' pattern instead of default param
  in platform_cli.py and all 3 delegate_response.py mirrors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix WebRTC session failures: set purpose='session' on all signaling paths

connectToWorker() was creating FirestoreWebRTCSignaling with the default
purpose='transfer', but Phase 9 changes made the responder only accept
purpose='session'. This caused all outbound WebRTC sessions to be silently
rejected, transitioning to 'failed' state.

Also adds onPeerDisconnected callback to PeerSessionManager so
SwarmCoordinator can clean up connectedWorkers when sessions fail after
initial connection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Wire WebRTC session lifecycle to connectedWorkers

After removing PeerConnectionManager (TCP), connectedWorkers was never
populated because handlePeerConnected/handlePeerDisconnected were only
called from the old TCP delegate callbacks.

Fix: Add registerWebRTCPeerAsConnected() helper that builds a
ConnectedPeer from Firestore worker data and calls handlePeerConnected.
Wire it into all three WebRTC session establishment paths:
- connectToWorker() (outbound manual connect)
- start() auto-connect loop (outbound during swarm start)
- onSessionAccepted callback (inbound from responder)

Also trigger handlePeerDisconnected when the MCP data channel closes
(detected in makeWebRTCListenTask when channel.isOpen becomes false).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 5 — GoDoc, polished README, presentation slides

- Add package-level doc comments to all 5 internal packages
  (engine, experiment, k8s, siem, report)
- Polish README.md: architecture ASCII diagram, badges,
  full Makefile reference, coverage table, proper quickstart
- Create thesis/slides.md: 16-slide presentation deck covering
  motivation, architecture, MTTD, evaluation results, RQ answers,
  test coverage, limitations, future work, and demo script
- Fix go vet: restore missing import block in k8s/client.go

Phase 5 complete. All deliverables done:
  - go vet clean
  - 89.3% test coverage
  - GoDoc on all exported symbols
  - Slide deck ready

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Networking cleanup: delete TCP fallback, fix continuation races, simplify WebRTC

Phase 4: Fix continuation races
- Replace waitForManifestAck() timeout race with task-group pattern
- Replace sendRequest() timeout race with task-group pattern in WebRTCMCPTransport
- Replace sendDirectCommandAndWait() timeout race with task-group pattern

Phase 7: Bounded collections
- Cap respondedOfferFingerprints at 500 entries with oldest-first eviction

Phase 8: Delete TCP fallback (~627 LOC removed)
- Delete PeerConnectionManager.swift entirely
- Extract ConnectedPeer struct to its own file (used by 7+ files)
- Remove all connectionManager references from SwarmCoordinator
- Rewrite connect/send methods to use WebRTC via Firestore signaling
- Replace TCP peers in heartbeat with WebRTC-only
- Update SwarmToolsHandler to accept peerId instead of address/port

Phase 9: Delete one-shot WebRTC codepath
- Simplify WebRTCSignalingResponder to only handle purpose=session
- Remove ping/transfer purpose handling, activeServeTask, dataProvider
- Remove WebRTCTransferDataProvider conformance from SwarmCoordinator
- Rewrite swarm.webrtc-ping to use PeerSession heartbeat RTT
- Remove import WebRTCTransfer from Shared/ code

Net: -617 LOC, cleaner architecture, no more TCP/WebRTC dual-stack

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: rename helix_search_log to helix_search and clean up internal naming

- Rename MCP tool from helix_search_log to helix_search for better discoverability
- Rename SearchLogResult class to SearchResult
- Fix error message to say 'Failed to search' (not 'Failed to search log')
- Update all references in CI guide, tests, README, and CLI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update test counts to 376, note Op2 partial eval complete

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

SKA Op2 partial eval + 14 new coverage tests (376/376)

Op2 partial eval: when formula-expression has known cape, resolve at
analysis time (inline/memo/loop/fresh-scan). Fixes run_nomm1 NOMM_2
handler for ax==0 Op2-originated DS2 nodes.

New SKNOCK test coverage:
- Op3 (cell?): YES and NO cases
- Op6: computed condition via op3, computed eq via op5
- Op7: triple inc (nested compose)
- Op8: push-then-inc, pin-and-eq, pin-neq
- Op10: edit tail
- Op11: dynamic hint
- Op2: chained inc, const-sub inc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(services/api): add Fastify REST API service for Ako LMS

- Zod-validated env config (JWT_SECRET required, no default)
- pg Pool with withTransaction helper
- Plugins: Redis (ioredis), JWT auth (@fastify/jwt augmentation), RBAC, tenant
- NATS publisher + outbox poller (topic/published_at columns)
- Global rate-limit, CORS, Swagger/UI
- Routes: health, auth (token/register/refresh/me), tenants, users,
  roles, courses (sections/modules/enrolments/gradebook), cohorts,
  forums (threads/posts/reactions), assignments (submissions/feedback),
  gradebook, messages, notifications, files, lti, scim 2.0
- Multi-stage Dockerfile
- SQL aligned to actual schema (files.owner_id, lti_registrations.issuer/keyset_url,
  assignment_submissions UNIQUE(assignment_id,user_id),
  assignment_feedback.teacher_id/comments, conversations.convo_type,
  outbox_events.topic/published_at, post_reactions without tenant_id)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4 complete: full thesis first draft (5 chapters, ~12,000 words)

thesis/chapter1-introduction.md (~3,000 words):
- Motivation, research questions, chaos engineering background
- Kubernetes security model (PSA, NetworkPolicy, Falco)
- Literature review: Chaos Mesh, kube-bench, Gremlin, Trivy, gap analysis
- Ethical considerations

thesis/chapter2-methodology.md (~2,000 words):
- Design science research approach
- 5 Whys RCA motivating requirements
- MoSCoW requirements (Must/Should/Could/Won't)
- Technology selection rationale (Go, Kind, Calico, Falco, Helm)
- Experimental design (golden vs misconfigured cluster)
- Validity threats (internal, external, construct)

thesis/chapter3-architecture.md (~3,500 words):
- Full system architecture diagram
- All 5 internal packages documented
- PSA admission edge case implementation
- AlertStore thread-safety and ClearRule design
- MTTD calculation rationale
- Falco Helm chart compatibility issues and fixes
- 27-test testing strategy

thesis/chapter4-results.md (~2,500 words):
- RQ1: 100% detection, two control layers validated
- RQ2: ~4,200x faster than manual, reproducible
- RQ3: MTTD architecture sound; null in local runs (deployment constraint)
- Threats to validity: network-egress Kind limitation, sample size

thesis/chapter5-conclusion.md (~1,000 words):
- Contributions summary
- Limitations
- Future work: expanded experiments, cloud SIEM, parallel execution
- Reflection on chaos engineering applied to security

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace empty mock classes with vi.fn() to satisfy S2094

Empty class stubs (class {}) still trigger S2094 'Unexpected empty class'.
Replace with vi.fn() which serves as a constructor mock without needing
a class declaration at all.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve 13 remaining SonarQube issues (post-rescan)

- Remove empty constructors from vi.mock class stubs (S2094/S6647)
  in client.test.ts, archived.test.ts, home.test.ts
- Remove unnecessary type assertions in home.test.ts it.each (S4325)
- Flip negated ternaries in client.ts and stats.ts (S7735)
- Reduce showQuiz cognitive complexity 22→~13 by moving ExitPromptError
  handling into askQuestion/askNextAction helpers, which now return null
  on user exit instead of throwing up to the main loop (S3776)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: eliminate code duplication to pass SonarQube quality gate

- Extract runStatsAndCapture helper in stats.test.ts to deduplicate
  showStats boilerplate repeated across 5 'with history' tests
- Convert computeReturnStreak tests to it.each to remove overlapping
  structural patterns flagged by CPD
- Collapse 3 identical AUTH error tests in client.test.ts into it.each
- Merge 2 identical routing tests in home.test.ts into it.each
- Convert slugify.test.ts single-assertion tests to it.each table

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate ReDoS-vulnerable regex patterns (S5852)

- client.ts: replace /\s*```$/i with string-based fence stripping
  to avoid O(n²) backtracking on whitespace before end-of-string
- slugify.ts: replace /-+$/ alternation with linear character iteration
  to avoid super-linear backtracking on unanchored quantifier

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
